items.rationale: Sees blockchains as the ideal architecture for an open, programmable IP layer linking real-world IP to on-chain licensing/remixing — especially urgent in the generative-AI era. His single most superlative excitement.
items.age_note: First stated Aug 2024 (Story Protocol Series B); reinforced June 2025 in AI×crypto roundup — current.

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol.
Blockchains are already being used to establish open, public, and programmable IP layers that provide immutable proof of provenance

items.rationale: Believes cheap transactions unlock ubiquitous digital collecting, identity, and brand-building (the 'NFT staircase': ownership, utility, identity). He actively lives this thesis through his own on-chain art.
items.age_note: Consistent thesis from The Everything Token (2024) through 2025; persistent and current.

citations.quote: once transaction fees got small enough, blockchains would enable all manner of low-cost digital collecting experiences.
that's three steps of the NFT staircase -- ownership, utility, and identity -- within fewer than 60 seconds

items.rationale: Argues crypto provides the interoperability, payment rails, and guardrails AI needs — portable LLM context, agent-to-agent payments, future-proofing vibe-coded software, and IP provenance.

citations.quote: the ways crypto will organize, complement – and occasionally tame – modern AI.
portable chatbot context... forwards-compatible digital identity... future-proofing vibe-coded software... marketplace guardrails for AI agents

items.rationale: Core thesis that digital ownership drives community cohesion and shared value creation, and that 'network' tokens (vs company-backed) are a distinct, legitimate value-and-legal category.
items.age_note: Long-running (2024 book) through token-classification framework Mar 2025; current.

citations.quote: digital ownership enabled by blockchains drives users to build a personal attachment with a given brand or platform
Despite what some may claim, not all tokens are created equal.

items.rationale: In a world of AI fakes, sees user-owned, interoperable, forwards-compatible on-chain IDs (a 'digital driver's license') as the answer for authenticating to any current or future platform.

citations.quote: we need proof of personhood and identity on the Internet – stat! Secure, interoperable blockchain-based IDs are the answer.

items.rationale: Believes well-functioning crypto markets need clear, enforceable rules protecting property rights, transparency, and fair competition; pro principles-based regulation and pro-legislation (FIT21).
items.age_note: From FIT21 advocacy (2024) to FT op-ed Aug 2025; most recent stance, current.

citations.quote: Well-functioning markets need rules – and crypto is no different.
FIT21 is a huge step forward for blockchain and crypto technology, QED.

items.rationale: Credits the proliferation of high-throughput L2s for making low-cost on-chain experiences real; he mints almost exclusively on L2s/Ethereum-adjacent rails.
items.age_note: Oct 2024 State of Crypto; consistent through 2025 minting practice.

citations.quote: The proliferation of high-throughput "Layer 2" blockchains over the past year and a half has made this a reality

items.rationale: Wants competition in social media and sees decentralized protocols (with data ownership and wallet portability) as the route, tying to his research on platform competition and misinformation.
items.age_note: Sept 2024; related content into Jan 2025, not strongly restated since — persisted.

citations.quote: competition in the social media market is a big part of why I'm excited about decentralized social media protocols

items.rationale: Long fascinated by health-data marketplaces (since work with Gary Becker); sees blockchain architecture as a way past their infrastructure and access barriers.
items.age_note: Stated once (Sept 2024 Amino Chain); not restated — persisted, ~12 months old.

citations.quote: Many ideas for these marketplaces have been explored, but they've faced fundamental infrastructure and access challenges -- which @Amino_Chain is using blockchain architecture to hopefully solve

items.rationale: Views enforcement-driven regulation and lack of clear rules as actively harmful — driving legitimate businesses and consumers away and failing to support high-quality actors.
items.age_note: Aug 2024 stance reinforced in Aug 2025 FT op-ed; current.

citations.quote: regulation-by-enforcement doesn't help!
Without basic structure and protections, uncertainty and inefficiency will drive away legitimate businesses and consumers.

items.rationale: Concerned that treating all tokens as equivalent muddies economic and legal treatment; pushes a framework separating company-backed from genuine network tokens.

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens are *not* network tokens. Here's a framework for understanding the difference

items.rationale: Warns that fixing supply/emission entirely up front (the Bitcoin precedent) is usually wrong; monetary policy should evolve with the ecosystem.

citations.quote: With token design, monetary policy evolving over time should be the default.

items.rationale: Dismissive of memecoins as low-value crypto froth, likening them to a chain-letter phenomenon rather than durable value creation.
items.age_note: Stated once Apr 2024, never restated (~18 months old); also avoids attaching memecoins to his own NFT giveaways/POAPs in 2025.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.

items.rationale: Flags that 'master account' / portable-identity visions require finer permissioning so users control which apps see which data — currently an unsolved blocker.

citations.quote: we need solutions for finer-grained permissioning so that people can choose which applications see which parts of their account information.

items.rationale: Sees creator royalties as a key NFT value proposition that remains technically hard to enforce — an open, unresolved design problem.

citations.quote: Creator royalties are a key value proposition of NFTs, but they've faced significant implementation challenges.

items.rationale: His default settlement and minting layer; celebrates the network's milestones and benefits from low gas + the L2 ecosystem built on it. Ecosystem preference, not an explicit price call.
items.age_note: Consistent throughout corpus; most recent positive note July 2025.

citations.quote: Happy Birthday Ethereum!!! Today's "45 squared" edition celebrates the 10th anniversary of the launch of the Ethereum network.

items.rationale: Not bearish on BTC as an asset, but repeatedly cites the 'Bitcoin precedent' of fixing tokenomics up front as a design anti-pattern others wrongly copy.
items.age_note: Sept 2024 design critique; narrowly scoped to tokenomics philosophy, not price.

citations.quote: Owing in part to the Bitcoin precedent, many projects try to fix their entire tokenomic design ... up front. But that's rarely the right path.

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish on crypto as technology infrastructure — tokens, NFTs, programmable IP, identity, and AI×crypto — and optimistic that sound regulation will expand the space. But he is an economist/market-designer, not a trader: he offers no tactical price/timing calls, attaches frequent 'not investment advice' disclaimers, and his optimism is about adoption and infrastructure maturation over years, conditioned on better rules and privacy/permissioning solutions.

notes: Kominers' corpus is dominated by recreational math, puzzles, and his own on-chain art ('45 squared', πdentities), plus a16z-crypto thought-leadership threads and public-health/economics commentary — not trading or token-price views. His 'market view' is therefore structural and infrastructure-centric, not tactical. Views are remarkably consistent over the 18-month span with no reversals; where stances recur (IP, regulation, AI×crypto) the most recent statements (2025) reinforce rather than contradict earlier ones. Specific-token excitement is weak by design: he deliberately avoids endorsing individual tokens and repeatedly disclaims investment advice, so ETH (ecosystem-preference) and BTC (design-philosophy critique) are inferred from infrastructure usage and commentary rather than asset calls. Strongest single signal: Story Protocol / programmable IP as the infrastructure innovation he's most excited about in 15 years.
